segments.text: The burden of the wilderness of the sea. As whirlwinds in the South sweep through, it comes from the wilderness, from an awesome land.
A grievous vision is declared to me. The treacherous man deals treacherously, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, Elam; attack! I have stopped all of Media’s sighing.
Therefore my thighs are filled with anguish. Pains have seized me, like the pains of a woman in labor. I am in so much pain that I can’t hear. I am so dismayed that I can’t see.
My heart flutters. Horror has frightened me. The twilight that I desired has been turned into trembling for me.
They prepare the table. They set the watch. They eat. They drink. Rise up, you princes, oil the shield!
For the Lord said to me,
Go, set a watchman. Let him declare what he sees.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: When he sees a troop, horsemen in pairs, a troop of donkeys, a troop of camels, he shall listen diligently with great attentiveness.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: He cried like a lion:
Lord, I st and continually on the watchtower in the daytime, and every night I stay at my post.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Behold, here comes a troop of men, horsemen in pairs.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered,
Fallen, fallen is Babylon; and all the engraved images of her gods are broken to the ground.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You are my threshing, and the grain of my floor!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: That which I have heard from Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, I have declared to you.
The burden of Dumah. One calls to me out of Seir,
Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The watchman said,
The morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire. Come back again.
segments.speaker: The watchman

segments.text: The burden on Arabia. You will lodge in the thickets in Arabia, you caravans of Dedanites.
They brought water to him who was thirsty. The inhabitants of the land of Tema met the fugitives with their bread.
For they fled away from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the heat of battle.
For the Lord said to me,
Within a year, as a worker bound by contract would count it, all the glory of Kedar will fail,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: and the residue of the number of the archers, the mighty men of the children of Kedar, will be few; for Yahweh, the God of Israel, has spoken it.
segments.speaker: the Lord
